page: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by hardware instruction sets including AVX-512 (VNNI and BF16) and SHA_NI, reflected in strong Passmark extended instruction scores. Memory performance is highly efficient for cached small-block reads, placing in the top 25%, though uncached large-block read and write operations fall into the average tier. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier efficiency in single-threaded compression and small-model LLM inference, while medium-model LLM inference and database operations perform at average levels.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and high-speed DDR5 RAM, the c8a.4xlarge offers high resource density for CPU-bound tasks without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent performance, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that require sustained high CPU utilization. It is best suited for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, data compression, image processing, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside strong cached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput. Supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware accelerations, it excels in CPU-bound tasks. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for sustained compute workloads, making it an appropriate choice for web serving, data compression, image processing, and small-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization for high-performance CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core compute-optimized platform featuring high-frequency AMD processors and DDR5 memory for intensive CPU and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
AVX-512 and SHA_NI hardware acceleration support
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for web serving, image processing, and small-model LLM inference
